description: The four-term sum equals its sigma form at sampled x, e^x equals the infinite series, and d/dx e^x = e^x holds numerically
\sum with braced bounds _{n=0}^{\infty}, the counter n used in the term recipe, \frac{x^n}{n!} built cleanly
The page shows sigma = spelled-out sum (the point of the lesson), not just isolated formulas
Text and math read well; markers kept
